27659.title: Airplane crashes in Nigeria
27659.extract:
Saturday, December 10, 2005
 

A DC-9, operated by privately owned, Sosoliso Airlines, crashed in Port Harcourt, Nigeria, reportedly killing at least 103 people. Seven people are reported to have survived the crash.
Landing during an electrical storm, the plane overshot the runway and burst into flames. The aircraft broke into three main pieces, scattering passengers and wreckage.
Some 75 children returning home for the holidays are among the victims.


Sources
 Nigeria jet crash leaves 103 dead — BBC News, December 10, 2005
Austin Ekeinde. Plane crashes in Nigeria, at least 60 dead: official — Reuters, December 10, 2005
Daniel Balint-Kurti. Passenger jet crashes in southern Nigerian city — Associated Press, December 10, 2005
